content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES

KRAUEL & KRAUEL
GOVERNOR PARK
5090 SHOREHAM PLACE, SUITE 101
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92122-5934

ROGER W. KRAUEL                                                   (619) 455-9737

FRANCESCA MECIA KRAUEL                                            FAX: 455-1358


March 9, 1992

Via Fax

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95812-0807

Re:  Request for Opinion on Behalf of
     City Council Member Robert Chamberlin (City of Coronado)

Dear Commission:

The City of Coronado will soon consider a proposed program
regarding the designation of streets to carry the City's traffic
through various areas of the City.  This program will be the
Traffic Circulation Element of Coronado's General Plan.  For each
area of the City, alternative routes will be considered for various
levels of traffic.  Please provide your advice in the following
context:

Background

A.  Councilmember Chamberlin has a financial interest in real
property located within one of the areas ("SUBJECT AREA") to be
addressed in the program.  His real property:

     1.  Fronts on one of the possible routes ("ROUTE A") being
         considered for the SUBJECT AREA; and

     2.  Is within 300 feet of another possible route ("ROUTE B")
         for the SUBJECT AREA.

B.  The level of traffic on ROUTE A within the SUBJECT AREA will
affect Councilmember Chamberlin's real property interest in a
manner that will be distinguishable from the effect of traffic on
ROUTE A on the real property interests of the public generally.

C.  The level of traffic on ROUTE B within the SUBJECT AREA will
have an undetermined effect on Councilmember Chamberlin's real
property interest.

Fair Political Practices Commission
City of Coronado
March 9, 1992
Page 2


D.  Any financial effect from the selection of the level of
traffic for routes in other areas of the City will be shared by
Councilmember Chamberlin's real property interest and a significant
segment of the public generally.

Questions

1.  If the program is considered by the City Council on a City-
wide basis, can Councilmember Chamberlin participate?

2.  If the program is considered in segments (on an area-by-area
basis) by the City Council:

     a.  If Councilmember Chamberlin disqualifies himself from
participating in the selection of routes for traffic in
the  SUBJECT  AREA,  may  Councilmember  Chamberlin
participate in the selection of routes for traffic in
areas of the City other than the SUBJECT AREA?

     b.  Can Councilmember Chamberlin participate in the decision
regarding ROUTE B in the SUBJECT AREA so long as the
decision has no financial effect on his real property
interest?

     b.  Can Councilmember Chamberlin participate in the decision
regarding ROUTE B in the SUBJECT AREA if the financial
effect  on  his  real  property  interest  is  not
distinguishable from the effect on the real property
interests of the public generally?

     The proposed program will be pending before the City Council
in the very near future.  Your timely response will be of
considerable assistance.

                                        Yours truly,

                                        [signature]
                                        ROGER W. KRAUEL
                                        City Attorney
                                        City of Coronado

RWK:mar

cc:  City Manager
